title: OpenCode Desktop Resilience Architecture Map
scope: Map resilience architecture for safe/degraded mode

failure_points.failure_mode: Unhandled rejection or uncaught exception in Electron main process
failure_points.current_behavior: Logs [fatal] to console and calls process.exit(1). No recovery attempted.
failure_points.crash_radius: Entire application terminates. Renderer windows close. Sidecar is orphaned (utilityProcess may be killed by OS).

failure_points.failure_mode: Sidecar process fails to become ready within 60 seconds
failure_points.current_behavior: Rejects spawn promise with error. No automatic retry. The main process does NOT restart the sidecar.
failure_points.crash_radius: App sits at loading screen indefinitely. User must restart manually.

failure_points.failure_mode: Sidecar crashes after successful startup (while serving requests)
failure_points.current_behavior: No automatic restart. Health check polling (every 100ms) detects unhealthy state. Exit promise resolves; app continues but is non-functional.
failure_points.crash_radius: All server-dependent functionality dead. Renderer continues to poll health but never recovers. No user-facing error beyond stale UI.

failure_points.failure_mode: Sidecar does not respond to stop message within 6 seconds
failure_points.current_behavior: Force-kills the utilityProcess via child.kill(). No fallback if kill also fails.
failure_points.crash_radius: Process may linger. On relaunch, port may be occupied. Second launch may fail silently.

failure_points.failure_mode: PGlite constructor throws during database initialization (corrupt files, permissions, disk full)
failure_points.current_behavior: Caught by try/catch, wrapped in DatabaseInitError. Propagated up as unhandled rejection in sidecar. No repair attempt.
failure_points.crash_radius: Sidecar cannot start. App enters crash-detected safe mode on next launch (via .crash_lock file).

failure_points.failure_mode: PGlite SELECT 1 fails at runtime (corruption, disk error, memory exhaustion for :memory: mode)
failure_points.current_behavior: checkPgHealth() returns { status: 'unhealthy', message }. HealthRegistry.set() updates component status. No automatic repair.
failure_points.crash_radius: Global health endpoint (/global/health) reports unhealthy. Desktop health check sees false. App UI shows blocked server. No fallback mode triggered.

failure_points.failure_mode: A migration SQL statement fails during applyMigrations()
failure_points.current_behavior: No per-statement error handling. Error propagates uncaught. Sidecar exits. Only 1 migration in migration-pg/ folder vs 20 in legacy migration/ folder — schema may be incomplete.
failure_points.crash_radius: Sidecar startup fails. Database may be left in inconsistent state (partial migration applied).

failure_points.failure_mode: migration-pg/ has only 1 migration file but migration/ has 20 — schema gap
failure_points.current_behavior: Database schema may be incomplete. Tables expected by application code may not exist, causing runtime query failures.
failure_points.crash_radius: Gradual runtime failures in any code path that queries missing tables.

failure_points.failure_mode: applyMigrations() is defined at db.pg.ts:70 but never called in any runtime code path
failure_points.current_behavior: Migrations are never applied automatically. Database schema may be empty/outdated. Only called from migrate.ts CLI script, not from sidecar startup.
failure_points.crash_radius: All queries fail if required tables don't exist. App is non-functional.

failure_points.failure_mode: Electron IPC message delivery failure (ipcRenderer.invoke / ipcMain.handle)
failure_points.current_behavior: typedInvoke() wraps calls with .catch() returning structured error. If IPC is truly broken, the Electron process is already dying — no separate recovery.
failure_points.crash_radius: If main process crashes, entire app terminates. If renderer crashes, main process continues but UI is gone.

failure_points.failure_mode: Renderer JS thread blocked for 15+ seconds (infinite loop, heavy computation)
failure_points.current_behavior: createUnresponsiveSampler() detects and logs call stacks. No automatic recovery. User must manually force-quit or wait. Electron shows native 'not responding' dialog.
failure_points.crash_radius: UI frozen. Sidecar continues serving. Data may be lost if user force-quits.

failure_points.failure_mode: Uncaught error in renderer that escapes ErrorBoundary (e.g., in event handler outside component tree)
failure_points.current_behavior: AppErrorBoundary catches tree errors — renders ErrorPage with Sentry.captureException. recordFatalRendererError IPC logs to main process log file. Platform.restart() reloads window.
failure_points.crash_radius: Route-level isolation via AppErrorBoundary per route. Non-component errors may cause white screen or partial UI breakage.

failure_points.failure_mode: Plugin process crashes repeatedly
failure_points.current_behavior: MAX_CRASHES_BEFORE_QUARANTINE = 3. After 3 crashes, plugin is auto-quarantined. Quarantine persisted to JSON. Manual unquarantine available.
failure_points.crash_radius: Only the offending plugin is disabled. Other plugins and core app continue normally.

failure_points.failure_mode: Postgres connection pool fails to connect (DNS resolution, network, auth)
failure_points.current_behavior: PgPool.create() retries up to maxRetries (default 3) with exponential backoff (100ms base). Classifies errors: auth failures (28P01) → non-retryable; network errors (ECONNREFUSED, ENOTFOUND) → retryable.
failure_points.crash_radius: Node-postgres mode only. If pool creation fails after all retries, the sidecar cannot start.

failure_points.failure_mode: HTTP health check to sidecar /global/health fails (network, timeout, sidecar down)
failure_points.current_behavior: checkServerHealth() retries up to 2 times with progressive delay. Timeout 3s. Returns { healthy: false } if exhausted. UI shows server as blocked/offline.
failure_points.crash_radius: UI shows disconnected state. User can switch servers or retry. No automatic sidecar restart.

failure_points.failure_mode: Application exits abnormally, leaving .crash_lock file
failure_points.current_behavior: On next launch, crashDetected = existsSync(crashLock) → enters safe mode. Safe mode renders safe-mode.html with 8 diagnostic/recovery actions.
failure_points.crash_radius: User gets diagnostic UI instead of normal app. Can attempt repair actions or retry normal startup.

failure_points.failure_mode: DatabaseInitError thrown from PGlite init() propagates through Effect Layer construction in makeLocalPgAdapter()
failure_points.current_behavior: makeLocalPgAdapter() calls initPg() which throws DatabaseInitError. No catch in adaptor layer. Error propagates through Layer.effect/Effect.sync unhandled, causing ManagedRuntime.make(AppLayer) to fail with a Layer defect. Entire application crashes — not just DB.
failure_points.crash_radius: Application-wide crash. Effect's built-in defect reporting may print to console but no user-facing error or safe mode entry. Sidecar process dies.

failure_points.failure_mode: new Pool() in init(node-postgres branch) throws — no try/catch wrapping
failure_points.current_behavior: Unlike the PGlite path (which wraps in DatabaseInitError), the node-postgres path at db.pg.ts:51-56 has NO error handler. Error propagates uncaught through caller chain.
failure_points.crash_radius: Same as PGlite but without even the DatabaseInitError wrapper — raw Error propagation. App-wide crash.

failure_points.failure_mode: applyMigrations() fails during auto-apply in adapter layer, but .catch() silently swallows the error
failure_points.current_behavior: console.warn('[db] Failed to auto-apply migrations (non-fatal):', err). App continues with partial or empty schema. Subsequent queries against missing tables fail at runtime.
failure_points.crash_radius: Gradual cascading failures. No safe mode or degraded UI. User sees runtime errors in session/tool operations.

failure_points.failure_mode: Sidecar fails to become ready — startup promise rejects via awaitInitialization
failure_points.current_behavior: awaitInitialization() returns { ok: false, error } via withIpcResult. But loading.tsx:27 calls .catch(() => undefined) — error is swallowed. Loading screen shows indefinitely with no error UI. No timeout at the renderer level for the init step.
failure_points.crash_radius: User sees a 'Just a moment...' screen forever. No error message, no retry option, no safe mode transition. User must force-quit the app.

failure_points.failure_mode: ipc.timeout error code exists in IpcErr type union but is never produced by any code path
failure_points.current_behavior: No IPC call has a timeout. All timeouts are domain-specific (sidecar 60s, git 5s, health 3s). If the Electron main process hangs during an IPC handler, the renderer's invoke promise hangs indefinitely.
failure_points.crash_radius: UI freezes waiting for IPC response that will never arrive. No circuit breaker, no fallback.

failure_points.failure_mode: IPC calls in renderer use .catch(() => undefined) or .catch(err => console.error(...)) — errors are silently discarded
failure_points.current_behavior: When IPC fails (sidecar dead, network error, permission denied), the caller receives a structured IpcResult error but the renderer code at the call site discards it. No toast, no degraded UI, no retry.
failure_points.crash_radius: User sees stale or empty UI with no indication of failure. Features silently stop working.

failure_points.failure_mode: Runtime flag OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS is defined but NEVER consumed by any migration or adapter code
failure_points.current_behavior: Flag reads from env (skipMigrations: bool) but is dead — no code path checks it. Migrations always attempt to run regardless.
failure_points.crash_radius: No crash, but the flag is misleading — users can't disable migrations even with the env var set.

retry_mechanisms.name: sidecar_health_polling
retry_mechanisms.strategy: Poll /global/health every 100ms until healthy or sidecar exits. No max attempts — polls indefinitely until timeout or exit.

retry_mechanisms.name: pg_pool_connect_retry
retry_mechanisms.strategy: Effect.retry with exponential backoff (100ms base), maxRetries (default 3), isRetryableError classification

retry_mechanisms.name: llm_session_retry
retry_mechanisms.strategy: Exponential backoff (2s initial, 2x factor, 30s max). Reads retry-after headers. Classifies 429/503/500 errors. Free tier upsell message on exhaustion.

retry_mechanisms.name: tool_execution_retry
retry_mechanisms.strategy: Configurable per-tool: retry { times, backoff, onError }. Uses Schedule.exponential.

retry_mechanisms.name: server_health_retry
retry_mechanisms.strategy: Retry up to 2 times with progressive delay. retryable() filter excludes AbortError, TimeoutError. Falls back to { healthy: false }.

retry_mechanisms.name: database_transaction_retry
retry_mechanisms.strategy: Effect.retry({ while: (error) => error.isRetryable }) for serialization/deadlock errors

retry_mechanisms.name: plugin_crash_quarantine
retry_mechanisms.strategy: Crash counter per plugin. After 3 crashes, auto-quarantine. Persistent state in JSON.

retry_mechanisms.name: websocket_to_http_fallback
retry_mechanisms.strategy: After streamFailures > streamRetries, switches to HTTP fallback for AI model communication

safe_mode.description: Crash lock file detection + --safe-mode CLI flag. Renders safe-mode.html with 8 diagnostic/recovery actions: export_debug_logs, open_logs, repair_database, disable_plugins, disable_mcp, clear_stale_locks, reset_config, copy_diagnostic_summary, retry_normal_startup.
gaps: SafeModeAction handlers are stubs — most actions log 'safe mode action: <name>' without implementing the actual repair (e.g., repair_database has no implementation).
InitStep { phase: 'safe_mode' } type exists but is never emitted; safe mode is detected via window load (safe-mode.html) rather than init step protocol.

degraded_mode.description: InitStep type includes { phase: 'degraded_mode' } but this phase is never emitted by the main process. No degraded mode implementation exists.
gaps: Type declared but zero emit sites.
HealthRegistry supports 'degraded' status but health check results don't trigger degraded mode UI.
No partial-functionality path when individual components fail (e.g., DB down but file browsing still works).

health_registry.description: Effect-based HealthRegistry tracks component health (Healthy, Degraded, Down, Unknown). checkPgHealth() reports database status. /global/health endpoint returns component states.
gaps: Health registry data is not consumed by the desktop main process or renderer to trigger degraded mode.
Only database component registered. No sidecar process health, no LSP health, no file watcher health.

error_boundary.description: AppErrorBoundary wraps every route and major component. Captures to Sentry. Renders ErrorPage with 9+ formatted error types and recovery actions.

sidecar_restart.description: No automatic sidecar restart exists. If sidecar crashes after successful startup, the app is permanently degraded. Health check polling detects the failure but takes no recovery action.

offline_mode.description: No offline mode exists. All functionality requires a running sidecar/server.

recovery_paths.name: safe_mode_retry
recovery_paths.description: User can choose 'Retry Normal Startup' from safe mode UI. Removes .crash_lock file, calls app.relaunch() + app.exit(0).
recovery_paths.trigger: Crash detected on startup OR --safe-mode flag
recovery_paths.effectiveness: Works for transient failures (port conflicts, race conditions). Does NOT fix persistent issues (corrupt DB, bad config).

recovery_paths.name: error_page_restart
recovery_paths.description: ErrorPage includes 'Restart' button that calls platform.restart() — window.location.reload() in browser, app.relaunch() in desktop.
recovery_paths.effectiveness: Works for transient renderer errors. Does NOT fix main process or sidecar failures.

recovery_paths.name: error_page_export_logs
recovery_paths.description: ErrorPage includes 'Export Logs' button that calls exportDebugLogs() → file save dialog. Collects logs, crash dumps, net logs.
recovery_paths.effectiveness: Diagnostic only. Useful for support but no automatic repair.

recovery_paths.name: plugin_quarantine
recovery_paths.description: Auto-quarantines plugins after 3 crashes. Plugin disabled on next startup. Can be manually unquarantined.
recovery_paths.effectiveness: Prevents crash loops from bad plugins. Requires manual intervention to restore.

recovery_paths.name: update_restart
recovery_paths.description: Updater kills sidecar, installs update, relaunches app.
recovery_paths.effectiveness: Can resolve version-specific bugs but requires manual trigger.

recovery_paths.name: app_menu_restart
recovery_paths.effectiveness: Full app restart. Loses unsaved state.

recommended_architecture.summary: Introduce a 3-tier resilience model: Safe Mode (existing) → Degraded Mode (new) → Full Mode. Each component independently reports health. Degraded mode gates non-essential features when components are unhealthy. Add sidecar watchdog with automatic restart.

tiers.name: safe_mode
tiers.description: Existing. Triggered by crash detection or --safe-mode flag. No sidecar running. Diagnostic/recovery UI only.

tiers.name: degraded_mode
tiers.description: NEW. Triggered when sidecar is running but critical components report unhealthy (DB down, file watcher broken, LSP unavailable). App launches normally but non-functional features are gated.
tiers.entry_condition: server healthy BUT (DB unhealthy OR other critical component unhealthy)

tiers.name: full_mode
tiers.description: Existing normal operation. All components healthy.
tiers.entry_condition: server healthy AND DB healthy AND all critical components healthy

key_additions.name: sidecar_watchdog
key_additions.description: Monitor sidecar process and automatically restart on crash with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 1s/2s/4s). After 3 consecutive crashes, escalate to safe mode.

key_additions.name: degraded_mode_ui
key_additions.description: Render degraded-mode.html (or extend safe-mode.tsx) when in degraded state. Show which components are unhealthy and offer retry/ignore options.

key_additions.name: component_health_registration
key_additions.description: Extend HealthRegistry to track all critical components: PGlite, DuckDB, file watcher, LSP manager, MCP bridge. Report health via /global/health endpoint.

key_additions.name: init_step_degraded_emit
key_additions.description: Emit InitStep { phase: 'degraded_mode' } when server is healthy but components are not. Renderer transitions to degraded UI.

key_additions.name: safe_mode_action_implementations
key_additions.description: Implement the stub safe mode actions: repair_database (run VACUUM/REINDEX), disable_plugins (write quarantine state), disable_mcp (write MCP disable state), clear_stale_locks, reset_config.

key_additions.name: migration_auto_apply
key_additions.description: Call applyMigrations() during sidecar startup (currently defined but never invoked at runtime). Ensure migration-pg/ folder is populated with all required migrations.

existing_findings_referenced.summary: applyMigrations defined at db.pg.ts:25 but never called anywhere in runtime
existing_findings_referenced.relevance: Directly blocks DB schema initialization. If migrations aren't applied, tables don't exist → runtime failures.

existing_findings_referenced.summary: migration-pg/ has 1 migration vs migration/ has 20 — massive gap
existing_findings_referenced.relevance: Even if migrations were applied, only 1/20th of the schema would be created.
